mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equities by lowering discount rates and supporting risk appetite, so S&P 500 forward returns are positive when the policy rate is declining over the recent trend window.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day S&P 500 forward returns are not positive in more than half of periods where FEDFUNDS has declined for at least 2 consecutive monthly observations, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: An inflation acceleration signaled by CPIAUCSL above its recent pace increases the expected path of real rates and equity risk premia, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month when inflation surprises are persistent.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises > 0.2pp month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered inflation-acceleration episodes are followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the sign of the average forward return is positive across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate indicates deteriorating labor income and weaker future demand, so S&P 500 returns are negative over the next month when UNRATE breaks above its recent level and firms are likely to revise earnings expectations down.
failure_condition: Reject if periods with the specified UNRATE rise do not show negative average 20-day S&P 500 returns, or if positive returns occur in at least 60% of triggered episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in PAYEMS and RSAFS confirms broadening payroll income and retail spending, which supports near-term earnings revisions and lifts S&P 500 returns over the next month more than either series alone.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint effect of rising PAYEMS and RSAFS is not stronger than the single-series effects in walk-forward tests, or if the combined signal does not produce positive average 20-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent increase in INDPRO signals stronger industrial output and better cyclical revenue momentum, which should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day forward returns are not positive on average following sustained INDPRO uptrends, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in HOUST below its recent trend reflects housing weakness that precedes softer consumer demand, reduced cyclicals earnings, and negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 5% below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if housing declines do not precede negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if the triggered episodes produce positive returns in more than half of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A joint improvement in PAYEMS and a stable or falling UNRATE indicates labor-market breadth without deterioration in slack, which supports equity returns over the next week by signaling stronger immediate domestic demand.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS-UNRATE signal fails to generate positive average 5-day S&P 500 returns, or if the interaction term adds no predictive value over PAYEMS alone.

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth and retail sales both weaken, the market should discount slower aggregate demand and lower earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter in a late-cycle slowdown.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 declines on the latest quarterly print and RSAFS is below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if the joint GDP-retail weakness regime does not yield negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns, or if positive returns occur in a majority of triggered instances.
